summary: Foundation runtime remains within current smoke budgets; the A-pose Meshy player character now uses one combined character-debug runtime GLB, reducing duplicate runtime requests but still exceeding gameplay byte/triangle targets.
observations: Three selected GLB weapon models plus their shared texture total 164055B of source payload.
Debug memory metrics include weaponModelBytesLoaded.
Debug asset metrics now include weapon loadedAssetIds and assetLoadErrors.
Manual browser smoke at 844 x 390 observed about 57 FPS with VAULT active.
Latest 844 x 390 smoke observed about 56 FPS with SPARK active after shot feedback was added.
Coordinate HUD is DOM text updated on the existing 250ms HUD cadence.
assetBuild query rewriting changes public asset URLs but does not add GPU resources.
Playwright browser smoke passed all five viewport gates with serial workers.
qaCapture=1 enables preserveDrawingBuffer for QA readback only.
Movement uses a body-only nearby-tile resolver instead of a manifest-derived weapon footprint probe.
WeaponSystem wall avoidance adds one close-wall ray/probe path during update.
The level pinch fix removed six walkable cells and added no render payload.
The structural entry-width fix opened eight walkable cells and added no render payload.
weaponViewPose.ts adds camera-local shot-effect math and a rounded debug effectPose field.
Latest npm run validate:browser passed all five landscape viewport gates after the entry-width and shot-effect alignment changes.
The structural entry splitter fix opened five additional walkable cells and added no render payload.
Latest npm run validate:browser passed all five landscape viewport gates after the splitter validator and map widening changes.
Latest npm run validate:browser passed all five landscape viewport gates after adding body collision resolution and the keyboard wall-push route.
Per-weapon muzzle offsets are manifest data only and do not add render assets.
weaponShotMath.ts adds a few CPU-only scalar calculations to keep pitched tracer endpoints aligned with the reticle.
Latest npm run validate:browser passed all five landscape viewport gates after per-weapon muzzle and pitch-distance changes.
The production JS chunk reported by Vite is about 644.59 kB minified after the pitch-distance helper and still triggers the known large chunk warning.
The mobile zoom guard adds non-passive event listeners and debug counters only; it adds no GPU resources or per-frame render work.

Browser smoke now uses two Playwright workers and limits full movement/gesture interaction coverage to chromium-modern-phone-landscape; all five viewports still run boot, HUD, asset, canvas, cache, viewport-scale, and hold-fire checks.
Local npm run test:e2e measured about 19 seconds after the MVP split.
The 3.84-unit foundation walls reuse the existing instanced wall geometry path and add no new draw calls or textures.
Larger gun scale and centered per-weapon aim poses are manifest/math changes and add no new model payload.
Hold-fire aim adds one camera FOV blend, one viewmodel pose blend, and cadence-gated shooting while held.
The reticle icon is a CSS-only DOM control and adds no WebGL resources; the extra gun-cycle icon button was removed.
Latest production JS chunk reported by Vite is about 651.33 kB minified and still triggers the known large chunk warning.

Latest npm run validate:browser passed all five landscape viewports in 44.5 seconds with two workers and the QA restart loop after the steel texture, wall height, movement speed, and centered-gun changes.
Browser smoke now asserts render calls, triangles, geometries, and textures against the debug budget object.
The steel foundation texture pass uses three 1024 x 1024 derivative PNG textures totaling 764229B source payload and about 12MB decoded RGBA estimate.
The full-level roof adds one PlaneGeometry, one MeshBasicMaterial, and one draw call.
The heavy browser smoke project now restarts the app five times through the QA restart hook and rechecks loaded assets plus renderer budgets each time.
Health and enemy state add CPU/gameplay objects only; the player health bar is DOM HUD.
The current external enemies share one hit-proxy BoxGeometry and clone three loaded GLB templates.
Weapon damage reuses the existing shot cadence and adds an enemy raycast only while firing.
Latest npm run validate:browser passed after adding enemy health HUD integration and debug toggle checks.
Latest production JS chunk reported by Vite is about 662.88 kB minified.
Enemy GLB payload is 347884B and uses invisible box hit proxies.
Level-01 weapon payload is now 261443B after adding RIFT and TORCH.
Foundation audio payload is now 858752B after adding two SFX and a 48 second music loop.
Latest production JS chunk is about 667.68 kB minified.
Enemy placement now expands to 12 map-authored E markers, while repeated enemy visuals clone three shared GLB templates.
Enemy model source payload remains 347884B because the runtime loads one template per enemy asset definition.
Enemy AI is kinematic and transform-only: squared-distance tracking, simple seek/return/patrol movement, and tile collision resolution.
Debug enemy radius/front-cone visuals are prebuilt LineSegments and toggle with debug mode.
Latest npm run validate:browser passed all five landscape viewport gates after enemy movement and debug visuals were added.
Latest production JS chunk is about 673.35 kB minified.
Enemy hit proxies are now a shared 2.15 x 2.35 x 2.15 box so weapon rays better match the external monster silhouettes.
Enemy hit feedback adds one shared small sphere geometry and per-enemy additive material that only becomes visible briefly on damage.
Enemy overlap handling adds an O(n^2) separation pass across 12 living enemies; this remains acceptable for the MVP marker count.
Latest npm run validate:browser passed all five landscape viewport gates after enemy spacing, hit-proxy, hit-flash, and separation fixes.
Latest production JS chunk is about 675.88 kB minified.
Enemy GLB reuse now uses SkeletonUtils.clone, which is required for skinned monster visibility and does not increase network payload.
Debug snapshots now compute attachment/model bounds for QA; this is debug-readback work, not animation-loop rendering work.
Latest npm run validate:browser passed all five landscape viewport gates after skeleton-safe clone and model-bounds checks.
Latest production JS chunk is about 676.65 kB minified.
npm run qa:headed:enemies is a local-only visible Chromium check that reuses the production preview and adds no runtime game payload.
The favicon fix prevents browser 404 console noise in headed QA and adds no WebGL resources.
Foundation walkable tile count is now 1658 after opening the isolated right-side room.
Enemy projectiles use one shared SphereGeometry, one shared additive MeshBasicMaterial, and pooled meshes.
Projectile collision uses tile raycasts and flat segment checks against the player; no physics engine or mesh collision was added.
Latest npm run validate:browser passed all five landscape viewports after adding enemy projectiles; production JS chunk reported about 684.03 kB minified.
Increasing enemy movement speed by 25% changes transform update distance only; it adds no meshes, textures, or asset payload.
Projectile readability tuning keeps the same shared pooled sphere geometry/material path, with a larger radius and depth testing disabled.
Latest npm run validate:browser passed all five landscape viewports after the first-shot projectile timing and enemy-speed fix; production JS chunk reported about 684.30 kB minified.
Weapon and projectile SFX now use decoded Web Audio one-shot sources after gesture unlock; this creates one source/gain pair per play but avoids HTML media pool clipping for rapid fire.
The compressed MP3 ArrayBuffer is released after decode, so gameplay keeps decoded buffers plus the existing fallback media elements rather than retaining both raw fetch bytes and decoded buffers.
Enemy projectile sound reuses the existing RIFT MP3 with playback-rate/gain changes, adding no new network payload in this pass.
Latest npm run validate:browser passed all five landscape viewport projects after the decoded SFX pass; production JS chunk reported about 686.64 kB minified.
Music now shares the Web Audio graph as a decoded loop, replacing the separate HTMLAudioElement path without adding network payload.
Audio asset fetch/verification for music and SFX runs in parallel with Promise.all; the decoded music buffer is the only added long-lived audio buffer beyond existing SFX buffers.
Latest npm run validate:browser passed all five landscape viewport projects after the Web Audio music pass; production JS chunk reported about 688.31 kB minified.
The foundation metal texture pass keeps the same three texture slots and decoded texture estimate while increasing PNG source payload to 764229B, still under the 1 MB foundation environment gate.
Removing the runtime floor GridHelper eliminates the extra grid geometry/material/draw path that was only serving the old prototype look.
Latest npm run validate:browser passed all five landscape viewport projects after the foundation metal texture pass; production JS chunk reported about 687.61 kB minified.
Focused screenshot QA reported renderer calls 81, triangles 25702, geometries 30, and textures 53 with qaCapture enabled.
Meshy main-character headed QA reported 81627 triangles, 2 draw calls, 2 geometries, and up to 7 textures while loading tested clips standalone.
Rigged GLB is 10229184B and largest tested animation GLB is 10371892B.
The staged player-character source pack totals 113046180B and must not be added to initial gameplay loading.
The A-pose Meshy character debug runtime GLB is 10983096B with ten embedded animation clips.
Headed player-character QA reported 81375 triangles, 2 draw calls, 2 geometries, and 3 textures for the combined GLB.
The new character debug flow requests one model GLB path and no per-animation GLB runtime URLs.
npm run validate:assets reports 125309634B player character source payload after adding the A-pose reference/runtime metadata.
decisions: Preload the three initial weapon GLBs because the payload is small and weapon switching should be instant.
Use npm run validate:assets to catch missing files, size drift, and hash drift.
Do a production-preview FPS comparison before importing heavier enemy or building assets.
Keep shot collision as tile raycast until enemies/projectiles justify a broader collision system.
Keep coordinate reporting in the lightweight DOM debug HUD.
Use Playwright for correctness smoke, not as the final performance benchmark while qaCapture is enabled.
Keep the body collision resolver because it scans only nearby tiles and runs during movement input.
Keep the weapon wall-avoidance probes for now because they are map-sample based and low cost.
Keep shared shot-effect pose and shot-distance math unit-testable rather than adding more scene graph probes.
Keep structural entry splitter validation in build/test tooling because it has no runtime frame cost.
Keep zoom prevention out of the animation loop and assert it through debug counters in smoke QA.
Keep MVP smoke focused on one full interaction route plus all-viewport layout/boot checks until the game grows beyond prototype scope.
Keep hold-fire pose and FOV math in scalar helpers and existing scene objects.
Use renderer budget counters as the MVP gate until visible-device FPS profiling is repeated.
Keep the roof as one shared plane and keep floor, wall, and roof textures shared by material to avoid per-chunk texture duplication.
Keep external enemy render meshes separate from simple shared hit proxies.
Keep SkeletonUtils.clone for skinned enemy GLB reuse despite the small helper import because raw Object3D.clone can break visible skinned bodies.
Keep enemy render meshes separate from hit proxies and avoid mesh raycast collision for this slice.
Clone repeated enemies from shared GLB templates until level streaming or LOD requires a stronger pooling system.
Keep enemy steering direct and room-local for MVP; revisit navmesh/A* only when levels need pathing around multi-room obstacles.
Keep scene debug enemy visuals as lightweight lines and hide them with the debug toggle during normal play.
Keep enemy separation as a tiny runtime steering correction while the enemy count is low; revisit broader avoidance only when rooms contain larger groups.
Keep headed enemy visual QA as a local runner rather than a deploy workflow gate.
Keep enemy projectile visuals as pooled primitive placeholders until final external VFX assets are sourced.
Keep enemy projectile sound reuse temporary; add a dedicated generated SFX only when the audio budget and provenance ledger are updated together.
Keep music and SFX on the same Web Audio graph so browser autoplay/unlock behavior is centralized.
Keep the foundation metal texture pass at three shared textures until level streaming or level-specific kits justify more surface maps.
Keep player.hero.gadget-gremlin as source/provenance only until optimized, merged, or regenerated at a lower budget.
Require a single optimized runtime character package or animation retargeting path before gameplay use.
Accept the combined GLB for character debug preview only.
Keep gameplay and title/cutscene runtime integration blocked until a later optimization pass reduces the character payload or approves an explicit exception.
caughtIssues: Detached loaded weapon models need explicit disposal when the weapon system is torn down.
Initial weapon asset package missed the shared GLB texture dependency.
Dev-server smoke did not show a locked 60 FPS.
Cache-busted URLs can create separate browser-cache entries across builds.
Vite still reports the large chunk warning for the Three.js-heavy bundle.
Weapon avoidance is approximate and should be profiled if close-wall checks expand beyond map samples.
Vite's large chunk warning still needs a future code-splitting review before the asset roster grows.
The earlier all-viewport full interaction smoke repeated the same route and gesture work five times, which was disproportionate for MVP CI.
Continuous fire increases audio/effect churn by cadence, but current visible primitives are reused instead of recreated per shot.
The wider enemy hit proxy slightly increases raycast intersection volume, but candidate count is still limited to living enemies and only evaluated when firing.
Raw Object3D.clone(true) is not safe for these skinned enemy GLBs even though it appears cheaper; it can make the visible body fail while bounds still report.
Enemy offense can add many active meshes if future rooms stack enemies; debug projectile active counts should be watched.
Decoded Web Audio one-shot playback allocates small source/gain nodes per shot; sustained fire profiling should watch audio node churn on physical devices.
Meshy animation exports duplicate the full mesh per clip.
The gun-hold clip needs replacement before weapon/cutscene use.
The combined GLB reduces duplicated animation runtime loading but is still above the 6000000B target and 45000 triangle target.
risks: Future larger weapon sets should not all preload by default without level/load-group gating.
The current 57 FPS dev smoke needs a production-preview comparison before increasing scene complexity.
More complex future level geometry may require replacing the grid resolver with a navmesh, capsule/octree, or authored collision proxy system.
Future cache analysis should compare build ids to avoid mistaking intentional URL changes for duplicate asset leaks.
Future mobile profiling should verify the zoom guard does not reduce simultaneous move, aim, and fire throughput on physical devices.
Restore broader per-viewport interaction routes before beta/release gates or when a viewport-specific gameplay issue is suspected.
Sustained held-fire profiling still needs a physical mobile device pass.
Future profiling should compare visible FPS with the roof on/off if enclosure or texture fill-rate becomes a concern.
Memory-lifecycle QA should still add a deeper heap/GPU leak pass; the current restart smoke only watches debug renderer counters.
External enemy assets can quickly exceed the current lightweight enemy budget if each enemy ships unique GLBs, textures, or animations.
Debug enemy line visuals add draw calls while debug is visible; future debug geometry should stay lightweight.
Direct steering can fail in more complex multi-room layouts and may need a navmesh or A* path layer later.
The O(n^2) separation pass should be revisited if the level grows beyond a few dozen active enemies.
Future dedicated projectile/impact audio will increase payload unless it replaces the temporary RIFT reuse.
Decoded long music loops consume more runtime audio memory than streamed HTML media, so future longer tracks should be budgeted before increasing duration.

jsonForAgents.movementCollisionCost: nearby solid-tile circle resolver while movement input is active
jsonForAgents.weaponClearanceCost: one weapon wall ray/probe for visual viewmodel avoidance
jsonForAgents.shotEffectPoseCost: shot-time and short-lived visible-effect camera-local vector/scalar math
jsonForAgents.zoomGuardRuntimeCost: event listeners only; no animation-loop work and no render resources
jsonForAgents.holdFireAimCost: camera FOV blend plus existing viewmodel pose blend while held; no new WebGL resources
jsonForAgents.foundationRoofCost: one plane geometry, one mesh/material, one draw call

enemyProxyCost.geometry: one shared 2.15 x 2.35 x 2.15 BoxGeometry hit proxy
enemyProxyCost.materials: one small proxy material per enemy plus one additive hit-flash material per enemy
enemyProxyCost.raycastTiming: only while a weapon shot is traced

jsonForAgents.healthHudCost: dom-only player health meter updated on the existing HUD cadence

decodedSfxPlayback.sfxMode: Web Audio AudioBufferSourceNode per SFX play

jsonForAgents.enemyCollisionCost: one 2.15 x 2.35 x 2.15 invisible box proxy raycast candidate per alive enemy during weapon shots only
jsonForAgents.enemyMovementCost: kinematic seek/patrol/return using squared-distance checks, tile collision resolution, and low-count O(n^2) enemy separation
jsonForAgents.enemyRenderReuse: 12 runtime enemies clone three shared loaded skinned GLB templates via SkeletonUtils.clone
jsonForAgents.enemyDebugVisualCost: one prebuilt LineSegments radius/front-cone object per enemy, visible only when debug UI is on
jsonForAgents.enemyHitFeedbackCost: one shared sphere geometry plus one small additive material per enemy, visible briefly only on hit
jsonForAgents.enemyModelBoundsSnapshotCost: Box3 bounds checks run only when debug snapshots are requested
